essentials: agreements between enterprises, persons, or associations
cause or are likely to cause an appreciable adverse effect on competition (AAEC) in India

_suggested_edges.reason: Section 3 is part of the Competition Act, 2002.
Reasonable conditions for protecting IPR are exempt under Section 3.
Export-related agreements are exempt under Section 3.
The Competition Act, 2002 was enacted to replace the MRTP Act, 1969 and establish a modern competition regulatory framework in India.
_suggested_edges.target_title: Monopolies and Restrictive Trade Practices Act, 1969

title: Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India

metadata.case_name: Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India
metadata.significance: This judgment significantly expanded the scope of fundamental rights under the Constitution.
metadata.ratio_decidendi: The judgment expanded the interpretation of personal liberty to a broader, more substantive due process approach, strengthening the protection of individual rights against arbitrary state action.

_suggested_edges.reason: The case involved a challenge based on Article 14.
_suggested_edges.target_title: Article 14 of the Constitution of India

_suggested_edges.reason: The case involved a challenge based on Article 19.
_suggested_edges.target_title: Article 19 of the Constitution of India

_suggested_edges.reason: The case involved a challenge based on Article 21.
_suggested_edges.target_title: Article 21 of the Constitution of India

essentials: Exploitative/Discriminatory Practices: Imposing unfair/discriminatory conditions or prices (including predatory pricing) in the sale or purchase of goods/services.
Limiting Production/Technical Development: Restricting production, markets, or technical/scientific development to the prejudice of consumers.
Market Access Denial: Engaging in practices resulting in denial of market access, such as exclusive dealing or tying.
Leveraging: Using a dominant position in one market to enter or protect another relevant market.
Supplementary Obligations: Making contracts subject to acceptance of additional, unrelated obligations.

_suggested_edges.reason: Case discussed in relation to market access denial.
Case discussed in relation to supplementary obligations.
